title: dianthus integer subsp. minutiflorus
intro: Dianthus integer subsp. minutiflorus, also known as a small-flowered pink or a dwarf pink, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family, native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant is rich in bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. These medicinal properties contribute to the plant's health benefits, including reducing inflammation, protecting against oxidative stress, and supporting immune function. Dianthus integer subsp. minutiflorus can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, but further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and optimal usage.
